title: gibasis venustula subsp. peninsulae
intro: Gibasis venustula subsp. peninsulae, also known as Peninsular Gibasis, is a subspecies of the Gibasis venustula plant, a type of flowering plant native to the Philippines. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. Gibasis venustula subsp. peninsulae can be prepared for consumption in various forms, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, or used topically in creams and ointments.
